title: Typed entity read envelope (GET /api/v1/<entity>)
description: The shared SERVER->CLIENT envelope every typed entity read returns (CONSOLE-CONTRACTS.md section 3.3). `items` is an array of the per-entity item shape pinned by the sibling schemas in this directory (fleet, heartbeat, registry, ...). Query params convention for every entity read: `limit` (integer, default 200, max 1000), `cursor` (opaque, from a previous response's next_cursor), `since` (RFC 3339 date-time; only items updated at/after this instant), plus per-entity filters noted in each entity schema's description. Freshness is computed, never trusted (Rule 10): consumers derive staleness client-side from `freshness` (and per-item observed_at where present); silence never renders as health. Consumers MUST ignore unknown fields.

freshness.description: Read-level freshness metadata: where this view was served from and when it was last observed true. Per-item staleness (e.g. fleet observed_at, *_epoch fields) refines this per row.

observed_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC — when the served state was last observed/refreshed from its source of truth.

window_s.description: The staleness window (seconds) appropriate to this source, for client-side staleness derivation; null when the source has no defined window.

items.description: The page of entity items. Item shape = the entity schema for the endpoint (schemas/entities/<entity>.schema.json). Item shape per endpoint: CI validates each endpoint with a composed schema (this envelope with items.items = the endpoint's entity schema, e.g. fleet.schema.json).

next_cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor; present (non-null) when more pages exist. Pass back verbatim as `cursor`. null = last page.

total.description: Total matching items across all pages when cheaply known; null when the source cannot count without a scan.

truncated.description: True when limit or the server byte cap clipped the page — silent truncation is banned.
